There being no objections, the Court granted the accounting petitions (subject to review/modifcation of the orders). The Court will address the issue of the Conservator's fees in the conservatorship matter. The Court informed Counsel for the Conservatee that a separate petition is required for her fee request.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jul-01-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Debra Fox for and with Olga Vose. Mary Gemma O'Keeffe for and with Anna Melnik. Mark Shustoff Estate planning Attorney also present.

Ms. Fox stated that they had no problems with the changes requested and advised the Court that Ms. Melnik had spoken to another Attorney on June 7th and signed a revocation of the Trust. Ms. O'Keeffe stated that Ms. Melnik wanted 100% control of her money, that Ms. Melnik does not want the conservatorship and does not want the Trust under Court supervision and recommended approval of up to $10,000.00 per month allowance. The Court approved the Third Trust Amendment and a one time transfer to of $10,000.00 into Ms. Melnik's bank account. The Court will then see where things stand at the first account. The Court granted the petition for instrucitons (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jun-27-2011 Continued To Jun-29-11 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Housing/Relocation And The Physician'S Report
Off Calendar May-17-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Gemma O'Keeffe for and with Anna Melnik (Conservatee). Debra Fox for and Olga Vose (Conservatee/successor Trustee).

Ms. Fox reported that Ms. Vose found a suitable apartment for Ms. Melnik, that she was expected to move in this week and that Ms. Melnik was happy with the location. The Court agreed to leave the number of Caregiver hours to Ms. Vose's discretion. Ms. Fox will review the previous order with regard to the number of current care hours set to determine if an amended order is needed. The Court sees no need to keep the matter on calendar for further status.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lazatin.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-01-2011 Granted Per Written Order

Petition(1) To Confirm Trust Amendment; (2) For Instructions Regarding Payment Of Care Provider From Trust
Off Calendar Feb-23-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Shustoff for and with Anna Melnik for Estate planning purposes only. Mary Gemma O'Keeffe for and with Anna Melnik. Debra Fox for and with Olga Vose. Valentina Grabovskaya in Propria Persona. Valentino Sukhorukov in Propria Persona.

Ms. Fox and Ms. Grabosvskaya will work on providing an estimate of mileage on a daily basis to the Court. Discussions were held with regard to raising Ms. Melnik's monthly allowance, a decrease in care hours, the difficulties Ms. Melnik is having in the new apartment and Ms. Melnik's display of fear. The Court would like to see a medical or psychological evaluation. The Court denied the request to increase the monthly allowance, approved the Trust amendment and the caregiver fees with the exception of the transportation fee request subject to a further declaration (subject to review of the order). Caregiver hours are reduced to six hours a day seven days a week subject to revision upon any change in circumstances. Ms. Melnik also expressed an interest in relocating. Status of housing/relocation and the Physician's report was set for May 17, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1227-1229 23rd Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94122)
Off Calendar Nov-17-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Debra Fox for Olga Vose (also present).

The sale was confirmed at $865,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth on the report of sale (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $815,000.00 is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Authority To (1)Sell Real Property Located At 1227-1229 23rd Avenue, San Francisco; And (2) Enter Into An Exclusive Right-To-Sell Agreement
Off Calendar Jun-17-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello for and with Olga Vose. Anna Melnik (Conservatee) also present.

The Court raised questions as to the contract with the Real Estate Agent having blank sections with regard to persons name having exclusive right to sell the property and court confirmation of the sale being required. Ms. Robello stated that those issues would be addressed prior to signing of the contract, that Ms. Vose would be selling the property with authority as the Successor Trustee, that notice was sent to Ms. Melnik and to Ms. Baresse (Ms. Melnik's Counsel), and that Ms. Vose also spoke to Ms. Melnik about the petition to sell the property. The Court granted the petition (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Petition For Court Supervsion Of Conservatee'S Trust, To Confirm Vacancy In The Office Of Trustee, And For The Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-06-2010 Text Ruling

Petition For Court Supervision Of Conservatee'S Trust, To Confirm Vacancy In The Office Of Trustee, And For The Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-06-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PTR-09-293072 (Conservatorship of Anna Melnik).

Appearances: Court-Appointed Counsel Irene Barrese for and with Anna Melnik (Conservatee).
Sheila Robello for and with Olga Vose (Temporary Conservator and Temporary Trustee).

Ms. Barrese stated that the appointment of a Conservator appears to be in order. Ms. Robello submitted on the pleadings. Ms. Vose stated that she was willing to serve. Ms. Melnik presented her concerns for the record. The Court granted the petition appointing Olga Honchariw Vose as the Conservator of the Person and Estate of Anna Melnik (order signed).

Further orders:
1. Compliance Status Hearing - 8/18/2010, 9:00 a.m., Dept. 204:
2. Assessment Fee - $725 is charged but deferred.
3. The Certificate of Completion of the conservatorship class shall be filed by 11/5/10.
4. First Account & Status Report - filed one week before July 13, 2011.
Bond is wiaved.
The Court stated that it wants to see the Inventory and Appraisal before it will consider the request to waive the accountings and that Ms. Robello may proceed by ex-parte to make that request in the future.

The Court addressed the Trust petition. There being no comments by Counsel, the Court granted the petition bringing the Trust under court supervision and appointing Olga Honchariw Vose as the Successor Trustee (order signed).

Further orders:
1. Status on the First Account - 7/13/2011, 9:00 a.m., Dept. 204.
Bond is waived.
Ms. Barrese is discharged.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#11312. Court Investigator: Probate Director Mary Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Order Confirming Olga Honchariw Vose As Temporary Successor Trustee On The Resignation Of The Acting Temporary Trustee And Waiving Trustee'S Bond
Off Calendar Apr-13-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PCN-09-293072 Conservatorship of Anna Melnik.

Appearances: Irene Barrese for and with Anna Melnik (Conservatee). Sheila Robello for and with Olga Honchariw Vose (Temporary Conservator/Temporary Trustee).

Ms. Robello stated that she spoke to former Counsel and to the former Temporary Conservator, and that they both agreed to the petitions and had no opposition. Ms. Barrese stated that it was in the best interest of Ms. Melnik. The Court stated that it was appropriate to grant the appointment of Ms. Vose as the Temporary Successor Conservator and as the Temporary Successor Trustee. All matters are to be heard on May 6, 2010 at the same time as the general petition. The Court granted both petitions (subject to review of the orders).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Court Supervision Of Conservatee'S Trust, To Confirm Vacancy In The Office Of Trustee, And For The Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-25-2010 Continued To Mar-30-10 At 11:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case no. PTR-09-293072 (Conservatorship of Anna Melnik) and heard by Judge Mary Wiss.

Appearances: Court-Appointed Counsel Irene Barrese for and with Anna Melnik (Conservatee) assisted in Russian-Ukranian/English translation by Olga Honcharin-Vose (Friend). Richard Grabstein also appearing for Anna Melnik. Lillian Ng for and with Christine Witherspoon (Temporary Conservator). Edith Chan of Adult Protective Services. Jonathan Ferdon and Marianne Warren for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court stated that it had reviewed the report and inquired whether the parties wished to confer outside the presence of the Court. The parties agreed, and the Court recessed. At the second call of the matter, a Court-Appointed Russian Interpreter appeared and was released. Mr. Ferdon stated that the parties had reached an agreement, that Ms. Witherspoon would withdraw her petition, that the petition will remain on but will be continued, and that Mr. Grabstein will help find a conservatorship attorney who will then file petition's for Successor Conservator/Trustee. In addition, he stated that Ms. Witherspoon's temporary appointment would continue until the other temp is in place, that the money coming in cannot be turned over, that the money will be held, and then turned over to Ms. Vose once appointed. Ms. Ng stated that the temp would be extended until the other appointment of the Temporary Successor Conservator/Trustee. The Court confirmed that Ms. Witherspoon's appointment continues until a Temporary Successor Conservator/Trustee is appointed. The Court continued both matters to March 30, 2010 at 11:30 am in department 204; matters may off calendar if the other temp is in place. Mr. Grabstein stated that he would help find Counsel to bring the petitions. Ms. Barrese is to remain on as Counsel.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli/Deputy C. Garcia.

Ex Parte Application For Order For Court Supervision Of Conservatee'S Trust And For The Appt Of Temp Successor Trustee Due To Vacancy
Off Calendar Jan-11-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 293072 (Conservatorship of Anna Melnik).

Appearances: Lillian Ng for and with Christine Witherspoon. Irene Barrese court appointed Counsel for Anna Melnik (Proposed Conservatee present). Oly Filatova for and with Anna Melnik. Edith Chan-Lee, Adult Protective Services (APS) Supervisor. Igor Belov interested Party in Propria Persona. Marina Brodskaya, Court Certified Translator.

Anna Melnik did not stipulate to Commissioner Kaplan hearing the matter as a Judge Pro Tempore. Therefore, the matter was heard by Judge Mary E. Wiss. All parties expressed concern about the Proposed Conservatee's well being and care needs, vulnerability to undue influence/elder abuse, provided information as to the circumstances leading up to the filing of the Petition for Appointment of a Conservator and what was in the best interest of the Proposed Conservatee. The Court granted the ex parte petition appointing Christine Witherspoon as the Temporary Conservator of the Person and Estate of Anna Melnik (signing the order for the same). The temporary conservatorship expires on February 25, 2010. Bond in the amount of $20,000.00 is required. The court also granted the Ex Parte Petition for Order for Court Supervision of Conservatee's Trust and appointed Christine Witherspoon as the Temprory Trustee (subject to the written order). The hearing on the trust matter was advanced from March 1, 2010 to February 25, 2010 to be heard with the conservatorship mattter.

Judge: Commissioner Sue Kaplan./Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
